items.md: # ( *First Revision* )

items.md: ## 0. F O R E W O R D
items.value: 0. F O R E W O R D

items.md: **0.1** This Indian Standard (First Revision) was adopted by the Indian Standards Institution on 7 October 1982, after the draft finalized by the Code of Practice for Power Installation and Maintenance Sectional Committee had been approved by the Electrotechnical Division Council.
items.value: 0.1 This Indian Standard (First Revision) was adopted by the Indian Standards Institution on 7 October 1982, after the draft finalized by the Code of Practice for Power Installation and Maintenance Sectional Committee had been approved by the Electrotechnical Division Council.

items.md: **0.2** Under Rule No. 35 of the Indian Electricity Rules, 1956, the owner of every medium, high and extra high voltage installation is required to affix permanently in a conspicuous position a danger notice in Hindi or English and the local language of the district, with the sign of skull and bones and of a type approved by the Electrical Inspector. The rule is reproduced in Appendix A.
items.value: 0.2 Under Rule No. 35 of the Indian Electricity Rules, 1956, the owner of every medium, high and extra high voltage installation is required to affix permanently in a conspicuous position a danger notice in Hindi or English and the local language of the district, with the sign of skull and bones and of a type approved by the Electrical Inspector. The rule is reproduced in Appendix A.

items.md: **0.3** It has been however observed that in different places, different sizes and designs of such notice plates have been in use and the need for standardizing the important features of such danger notice plates has been long since felt. This standard was therefore originally prepared in 1963, to cover Hindi version of the notice plates in consonance with the then existing rules.
items.value: 0.3 It has been however observed that in different places, different sizes and designs of such notice plates have been in use and the need for standardizing the important features of such danger notice plates has been long since felt. This standard was therefore originally prepared in 1963, to cover Hindi version of the notice plates in consonance with the then existing rules.

items.md: **0.4** This revision is being brought out to cover standardized features of notice plates in line with IE Rule 35 as amended as well as to lay down essential requirements and tests to ensure satisfactory functioning of danger notice plates in use. This revision also includes recommended equivalent to the word 'danger' in some of the regional languages for use in the respective states. Equivalents in other languages are under consideration.
items.value: 0.4 This revision is being brought out to cover standardized features of notice plates in line with IE Rule 35 as amended as well as to lay down essential requirements and tests to ensure satisfactory functioning of danger notice plates in use. This revision also includes recommended equivalent to the word 'danger' in some of the regional languages for use in the respective states. Equivalents in other languages are under consideration.

items.md: **0.5** This standard for the time being includes the requirements of danger notice in the form of plates only. It is, however, recognized that the use of danger notices in the form of stickers are also in vogue in the country in some States. The requirements of such danger notices in a form other than plates are under consideration for standardization.
items.value: 0.5 This standard for the time being includes the requirements of danger notice in the form of plates only. It is, however, recognized that the use of danger notices in the form of stickers are also in vogue in the country in some States. The requirements of such danger notices in a form other than plates are under consideration for standardization.

items.md: # 1. SCOPE
items.value: 1. SCOPE

items.md: **1.1** This standard covers danger notice plates to be displayed in accordance with Rule No. 35 of the Indian Electricity Rules, 1956.
items.value: 1.1 This standard covers danger notice plates to be displayed in accordance with Rule No. 35 of the Indian Electricity Rules, 1956.

items.md: # 2. DANGER NOTICE PLATES
items.value: 2. DANGER NOTICE PLATES

items.md: ## 2.1 Dimensions
items.value: 2.1 Dimensions

items.md: **2.1.1** For the purposes of this standard, two sizes of danger notice plates are recommended as below:
items.value: 2.1.1 For the purposes of this standard, two sizes of danger notice plates are recommended as below:

items.md:
* a) For display at medium voltage installations: 200 $\times$ 150 mm ( *see* Fig. 1 )
* b) For high and extra high voltage installations: 250 $\times$ 200 mm ( *see* Fig. 2 )
a) For display at medium voltage installations: 200 $\times$ 150 mm ( *see* Fig. 1 )
items.value: a) For display at medium voltage installations: 200 $\times$ 150 mm ( see Fig. 1 )

items.md: b) For high and extra high voltage installations: 250 $\times$ 200 mm ( *see* Fig. 2 )
items.value: b) For high and extra high voltage installations: 250 $\times$ 200 mm ( see Fig. 2 )

items.md: **2.1.2** All letterings shall be centrally spaced. The dimensions of the letters, figures and their respective positions shall be as given in Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 for medium voltage and high and extra high voltage installations respectively. The size of each letter in the word in each language, and the spacing between them for the purposes of scribing shall be so chosen that they are uniformly written in the space earmarked for them.

items.md: **2.1.3** The corners of the plate shall be rounded off.
items.value: 2.1.3 The corners of the plate shall be rounded off.

items.md: **2.1.4** The location of the fixing holes shall be left to the choice of the user.
items.value: 2.1.4 The location of the fixing holes shall be left to the choice of the user.

items.md: # 3. MATERIAL AND FINISH
items.value: 3. MATERIAL AND FINISH

items.md: **3.1** The plate shall be made from mild steel, at least 1.6 mm thick and vitreous enamelled white, with letters, figures and the conventional skull and cross-bones in signal red colour \[ *see* IS : 5-1978† ] on the front side. The rear side of the plate shall also be enamelled.

items.md: ## 6.0 General
items.value: 6.0 General

items.md: **6.0.1** In order to ensure that the notice plates conform to this specification, the following essential tests are specified. The number of samples to be tested shall be as agreed to between the supplier and the user.
items.value: 6.0.1 In order to ensure that the notice plates conform to this specification, the following essential tests are specified. The number of samples to be tested shall be as agreed to between the supplier and the user.

items.md:
**6.0.2** The following shall constitute the tests:
a) Visual examination,
b) Dimensional check, and
c) Test for wheather-proofness.
items.value:
6.0.2 The following shall constitute the tests:
a) Visual examination,
b) Dimensional check, and

items.md: **6.1 Visual Examination** — The samples of notice plates shall be examined visually for conformity to the various requirements of this standard in respect of the works and letters used, their relative position and size. The colour of the paint used shall be visually compared with the signal red colour specified in IS : 5-1978\*.
items.value: 6.1 Visual Examination — The samples of notice plates shall be examined visually for conformity to the various requirements of this standard in respect of the works and letters used, their relative position and size. The colour of the paint used shall be visually compared with the signal red colour specified in IS : 5-1978*.

items.md: **6.2 Dimensional Check** — The dimensions of the plate, its thickness and the size of lettering, figures, etc, shall be checked for conformity to Fig. 1 or Fig. 2 as applicable. All letterings shall be centrally spaced and shall conform generally to the stipulations in **2.1.2** to **2.1.4**.
items.value: 6.2 Dimensional Check — The dimensions of the plate, its thickness and the size of lettering, figures, etc, shall be checked for conformity to Fig. 1 or Fig. 2 as applicable. All letterings shall be centrally spaced and shall conform generally to the stipulations in 2.1.2 to 2.1.4.
